description: Corrected Adult Tachyarrhythmia With a Pulse FSM per AHA 2025 ACLS guidelines. Fixes: narrow/wide QRS sub-states now fully expanded (not final stubs), regularity branching added at every decision node, WPW/pre-excitation pathway added with AV-nodal-blocker contraindications, polymorphic VT → unsynchronized shock path, cardioversion energy levels per rhythm type, adenosine dosing, antiarrhythmic drug contraindications, and context extended with all required clinical fields.

thresholds._comment: Clinical thresholds per AHA 2025. Modify only under medical director review.

cardioversion_energy.polymorphic_vt_note: UNSYNCHRONIZED high-energy shock (defibrillation dose) — do NOT use synchronized cardioversion

context._comment: Runtime state — populated by the debriefing engine as events are processed from transcribed audio/video.

guards._comment: Guard definitions. Evaluated at the time the event fires. Violations push to context.deviations[].

adenosine_only_if_regular.description: Adenosine must NOT be given for irregular rhythms (e.g., Afib). It is only indicated for regular narrow or regular monomorphic wide QRS tachycardias.

deviation_if_violated.message: Adenosine given for an irregular rhythm. Adenosine is contraindicated in irregular tachycardias (e.g., Afib, polymorphic VT). It is only indicated for regular rhythms.

adenosine_only_if_narrow_or_regular_mono.description: For wide QRS, adenosine is ONLY appropriate if rhythm is regular AND monomorphic. Irregular wide QRS must NOT receive adenosine.

deviation_if_violated.message: Adenosine given for a wide, irregular, or polymorphic QRS tachycardia. AHA 2025: Adenosine for wide QRS ONLY if regular and monomorphic.

no_av_nodal_blockers_in_pre_excitation.description: AHA 2025 Class III — HARM: Adenosine, beta-blockers, non-DHP calcium channel blockers, and digoxin are strictly contraindicated in pre-excited Afib/flutter (WPW). They may precipitate VF.

deviation_if_violated.message: AV nodal blocking agent (adenosine, beta-blocker, calcium channel blocker, or digoxin) given in suspected pre-excited Afib/flutter (WPW). AHA 2025 Class III — HARM. Risk of VF.

no_amiodarone_in_pre_excitation_afib.description: AHA 2025: IV amiodarone must NOT be given for pre-excited Afib/flutter — it may increase ventricular response and cause VF.

deviation_if_violated.message: IV Amiodarone given for pre-excited Afib/flutter. AHA 2025 explicitly prohibits this. Use Procainamide or Ibutilide instead.

no_verapamil_diltiazem_wide_unknown.description: Verapamil and diltiazem are absolutely contraindicated in wide-complex tachycardia of uncertain etiology. Can cause hemodynamic collapse.
no_verapamil_diltiazem_wide_unknown.condition: NOT (event.drug IN ['verapamil', 'diltiazem'] AND context.qrs_duration_sec >= 0.12 AND context.rhythm_label IN ['unknown', null])

deviation_if_violated.message: Verapamil or diltiazem given for wide-complex tachycardia of uncertain etiology. AHA 2025: Absolutely contraindicated — risk of hemodynamic collapse.

procainamide_contraindications.description: Procainamide is contraindicated in prolonged QT and CHF.

deviation_if_violated.message: Procainamide given despite contraindication (prolonged QT or CHF). AHA 2025: Avoid procainamide in these conditions.

sotalol_contraindications.description: Sotalol is contraindicated in prolonged QT.

deviation_if_violated.message: Sotalol given despite prolonged QT. AHA 2025: Avoid sotalol in prolonged QT — risk of Torsades de Pointes.

polymorphic_vt_no_synchronized_cardiover.description: Polymorphic VT must be treated with UNSYNCHRONIZED high-energy shocks (defibrillation), NOT synchronized cardioversion. Synchronized cardioversion in polymorphic VT is unsafe.

deviation_if_violated.message: Synchronized cardioversion attempted for polymorphic VT. AHA 2025: Polymorphic VT must receive UNSYNCHRONIZED high-energy shocks (treated as VF). Synchronized cardioversion is unsafe.

sedation_before_cardioversion.description: AHA 2025: Sedate the patient whenever feasible before synchronized cardioversion. Document if sedation was withheld and why (e.g., critical instability requiring immediate shock).

deviation_if_violated.message: Synchronized cardioversion performed without documented sedation. AHA 2025: Sedate whenever feasible. If deferred due to critical instability, document reason.

adenosine_iv_push_route.description: Adenosine must be given as rapid IV push followed by a normal saline flush to ensure delivery to central circulation before the drug degrades.

deviation_if_violated.message: Adenosine not administered as rapid IV push with NS flush. AHA 2025: Adenosine has an extremely short half-life and MUST be given as a rapid IV push followed immediately by a NS flush.

cardioversion_synchronized_flag.description: AHA 2025: Ensure defibrillator is set to SYNCHRONIZED mode before shock delivery for cardioversion. Unsynchronized shock in regular tachycardia risks R-on-T and VF induction.

deviation_if_violated.message: Cardioversion shock delivered without confirmed synchronized mode. Risk of R-on-T phenomenon and VF induction.

actions._comment: What the engine updates in context when each event fires.
actions.record_vitals: Update context.heart_rate_bpm, context.qrs_duration_sec, context.rhythm_regularity, context.rhythm_label, context.qrs_morphology from event.
actions.record_instability_signs: Update context.instability_signs from event. Set context.hemodynamically_stable based on whether any instability sign is true.
actions.record_cardioversion: context.cardioversion_attempted = true; context.cardioversion_count += 1; context.cardioversion_energy_used_J = event.energy_J; context.cardioversion_result = event.result.
actions.push_deviation: Push { rule, severity, timestamp_ms, detail } into context.deviations[].

initial_assessment.description: Assess appropriateness of heart rate for clinical condition. Heart rate typically >= 150 bpm if tachyarrhythmia. Maintain patent airway; assist breathing as necessary. Oxygen if hypoxemic. Continuous cardiac monitoring, blood pressure, and oximetry. Establish IV access. 12-lead ECG if available.

check_hemodynamic_stability.description: Evaluate for persistent tachyarrhythmia causing instability: Hypotension? Acutely altered mental status? Signs of shock? Ischemic chest discomfort? Acute heart failure? If ANY instability sign is present → immediate synchronized cardioversion. If ALL stable → evaluate QRS width.

synchronized_cardioversion.description: UNSTABLE PATHWAY. Perform immediate synchronized cardioversion. Sedate whenever feasible without delaying procedure. For Afib/flutter: start at 200J biphasic. For narrow regular SVT or monomorphic VT: 100J biphasic. If rhythm is regular narrow complex, consider adenosine while preparing for cardioversion. CRITICAL: If polymorphic VT → use UNSYNCHRONIZED high-energy shocks (defibrillation dose), NOT synchronized cardioversion.

ADENOSINE_GIVEN._comment: Adenosine may be considered here ONLY if narrow regular complex, while defibrillator is being prepared.

UNSYNCHRONIZED_SHOCK_DELIVERED._comment: Used for polymorphic VT which must be treated as VF with defibrillation dose. No synchronized mode required.

post_cardioversion_assessment.description: Assess rhythm following cardioversion or defibrillation shock. Did rhythm convert to sinus? If yes → stable patient care. If no → refractory management.

refractory_tachyarrhythmia.description: Tachyarrhythmia persists after cardioversion/defibrillation. Consider: (1) Re-evaluate underlying cause. (2) Increase energy level for next cardioversion attempt. (3) Add or change antiarrhythmic drug. (4) Expert consultation strongly recommended.

UNDERLYING_CAUSE_TREATED._comment: After treating underlying cause (e.g., ischemia, electrolyte abnormality), reassess stability.

evaluate_qrs_width.description: STABLE PATHWAY. Classify QRS complex: Wide = >= 0.12 seconds. Narrow = < 0.12 seconds. This determines the downstream management pathway.

QRS_WIDE._comment: QRS >= 0.12 seconds. Assume VT until proven otherwise.

narrow_qrs_pathway.description: STABLE, NARROW QRS (< 0.12 sec). Must determine regularity next. Regular narrow → vagal maneuvers + adenosine. Irregular narrow → rate control, expert consult.

classify_narrow_regularity.description: Is the narrow QRS rhythm regular or irregular? Regular suggests SVT, AVNRT, AVRT, atrial flutter with fixed block. Irregular suggests Afib, atrial flutter with variable block, MAT.

narrow_regular.description: Regular narrow QRS tachycardia. Likely SVT (AVNRT, AVRT) or atrial flutter with fixed block. Step 1: Vagal maneuvers. Step 2: If persists → Adenosine 6mg rapid IV push + NS flush. Step 3: If persists → second Adenosine dose 12mg. Step 4: If persists → beta-blocker or calcium channel blocker + expert consult.

ADENOSINE_GIVEN._comment: First dose 6mg rapid IV push + NS flush. If no response after 1-2 min, give second dose 12mg.

narrow_regular_refractory.description: Narrow regular rhythm persists after vagal maneuvers and adenosine (up to 2 doses: 6mg then 12mg). Options: beta-blocker (metoprolol, esmolol) OR non-DHP calcium channel blocker (diltiazem, verapamil — ONLY if no pre-excitation, no wide QRS uncertainty). Expert consultation recommended.

narrow_irregular.description: Irregular narrow QRS tachycardia. Most likely: Atrial Fibrillation (Afib), Atrial Flutter with variable block, or Multifocal Atrial Tachycardia (MAT). CRITICAL STEP: Check for pre-excitation (WPW) on ECG — delta waves, short PR. If pre-excitation suspected → NO AV nodal blockers (adenosine, beta-blockers, CCBs, digoxin) — risk of VF. Treatment: rate control (if no pre-excitation) or procainamide/ibutilide (if pre-excitation). Expert consultation strongly recommended.

AV_NODAL_BLOCKER_GIVEN._comment: If pre_excitation_suspected is true, this fires a CRITICAL deviation.

narrow_irregular_rate_control.description: Irregular narrow QRS tachycardia WITHOUT pre-excitation (confirmed). Rate control strategy: beta-blocker or non-DHP calcium channel blocker (diltiazem, verapamil). If Afib with RVR and patient stable: rate control is first-line. Anticoagulation consideration per CHA2DS2-VASc. Expert consultation recommended for rhythm control decisions.

pre_excited_afib.description: Pre-excited Afib or Atrial Flutter (WPW pattern) — CRITICAL PATHWAY. AV nodal blockers are CLASS III — HARM (adenosine, beta-blockers, non-DHP CCBs, digoxin, IV amiodarone are all contraindicated). Acceptable options: (1) Electrical cardioversion (preferred if unstable), (2) Procainamide IV, (3) Ibutilide. Refer for EP evaluation and catheter ablation.

AV_NODAL_BLOCKER_GIVEN._comment: Any AV nodal blocker here triggers a CRITICAL deviation.

PATIENT_DETERIORATES_TO_UNSTABLE._comment: Any time a stable narrow QRS patient deteriorates to unstable, escalate to immediate cardioversion.

wide_qrs_pathway.description: STABLE, WIDE QRS (>= 0.12 sec). Assume VT until proven otherwise. Must classify by morphology (monomorphic vs. polymorphic) and regularity. CRITICAL: Verapamil and diltiazem are absolutely contraindicated in wide-complex tachycardia of uncertain etiology.

classify_wide_type.description: Classify the wide QRS tachycardia: (1) Regular monomorphic — likely VT or SVT with aberrancy. (2) Irregular/polymorphic — likely polymorphic VT or Afib with aberrancy/pre-excitation. If uncertain, treat as VT.

wide_regular_monomorphic.description: Regular monomorphic wide QRS tachycardia. Treat as VT. Options: (1) Adenosine — may be considered for diagnosis/treatment (regular + monomorphic only). Have defibrillator immediately ready. (2) Antiarrhythmic infusion: Procainamide (preferred if no CHF/prolonged QT), Amiodarone, or Sotalol (avoid if prolonged QT). (3) Expert consultation strongly recommended. (4) If deteriorates → synchronized cardioversion.

ADENOSINE_GIVEN._comment: AHA 2025: Adenosine considered only if regular and monomorphic. Defibrillator must be immediately available — adenosine can precipitate VF in VT.

VERAPAMIL_GIVEN._comment: Triggers CRITICAL deviation — absolutely contraindicated in wide QRS of uncertain etiology.

DILTIAZEM_GIVEN._comment: Triggers CRITICAL deviation — absolutely contraindicated in wide QRS of uncertain etiology.

wide_polymorphic_or_irregular.description: Irregular or polymorphic wide QRS tachycardia. May represent: Polymorphic VT, Torsades de Pointes (long QT), Afib with aberrancy, or pre-excited Afib (WPW). CRITICAL: Do NOT give synchronized cardioversion for polymorphic VT — treat as VF with UNSYNCHRONIZED high-energy shocks. Check QT interval: if prolonged QT → suspect Torsades de Pointes → Magnesium sulfate 1-2g IV. Check for pre-excitation (WPW). Avoid adenosine, beta-blockers, CCBs, digoxin, amiodarone if pre-excited.

PRE_EXCITATION_IDENTIFIED._comment: Wide irregular + pre-excitation = pre-excited Afib (WPW). No AV nodal blockers.

UNSYNCHRONIZED_SHOCK_DELIVERED._comment: Correct treatment for unstable polymorphic VT — defibrillation dose.

SYNCHRONIZED_CARDIOVERSION_ATTEMPTED._comment: CRITICAL deviation — synchronized cardioversion in polymorphic VT is unsafe.

torsades_de_pointes.description: Polymorphic VT with prolonged QT = Torsades de Pointes (TdP). Primary treatment: Magnesium Sulfate 1-2g IV. Correct underlying cause (hypokalemia, hypomagnesemia, drug-induced QT prolongation). Avoid amiodarone and sotalol (worsen QT prolongation). If unstable → UNSYNCHRONIZED high-energy shock (defibrillation). Expert consultation essential.

AMIODARONE_GIVEN._comment: Amiodarone contraindicated in TdP/prolonged QT — worsens QT prolongation.

deviation.message: Amiodarone given for Torsades de Pointes. AHA 2025: Amiodarone is contraindicated in TdP as it worsens QT prolongation. Use Magnesium Sulfate.

wide_polymorphic_ischemic.description: Polymorphic VT with normal QT and no pre-excitation. Likely caused by acute myocardial ischemia. Primary focus: identify and treat underlying ischemia/cause. If unstable → UNSYNCHRONIZED high-energy shock. Expert consultation essential.

PATIENT_DETERIORATES_TO_UNSTABLE._comment: Any stable wide QRS patient who deteriorates → immediate synchronized cardioversion (or unsynchronized if polymorphic VT).

rhythm_converted_care.description: Rhythm successfully converted. Monitor patient. Identify and treat underlying cause to prevent recurrence. 12-lead ECG post-conversion. Continuous monitoring. Expert consultation for recurrence prevention. If rhythm recurs → reassess from check_hemodynamic_stability.

cardiac_arrest_pathway.description: Patient has deteriorated to pulseless cardiac arrest. Hand off to AHA 2025 Adult Cardiac Arrest Algorithm (aha_2025_cpr_engine). This FSM terminates and the cardiac arrest FSM takes over.
cardiac_arrest_pathway._comment: Trigger aha_2025_cpr_engine FSM with ARREST_RECOGNIZED event.

terminated.description: Patient stabilized or case closed. Debriefing session complete.

deviation_severity_levels.CRITICAL: Directly contradicts AHA 2025 guideline with potential for patient death. Must be addressed in debriefing.
deviation_severity_levels.HIGH: Significant protocol deviation. Clear guideline violation. Requires debriefing discussion.
deviation_severity_levels.MEDIUM: Notable deviation. May impact patient outcome. Recommend debriefing review.
deviation_severity_levels.LOW: Minor deviation or soft recommendation. Discuss as improvement opportunity.
deviation_severity_levels.ADVISORY: Informational. No clear protocol violation but worth noting.
